method: COMMENT_EVALUATION batch 2 (researcher-approved 2026-06-02). 144: M38 already covers atonement<-mercy (finding 21139) -> fold for M38 + SD_CLUSTER reciprocal to M05 (mercy's home). 127/146/147/148: division (reg 52) and yielding (reg 180) have no cluster; researcher homes both to M44 (Relational Disposition) -> SD_CLUSTER pointer + fold. Completes the A7 DIMENSION_REVIEW batch.

new_flags.description: From M38 comment-eval (DIM-111-001, wa_session_b_findings id 144). Mercy (reg 111; clustered mainly to M05 Love/Compassion/Kindness) is the divine inner relational disposition; atonement (kipper/kaphar/kapporet; hilasmos/hilastērios — M38 char 125/126) is its structural MECHANISM — how God's mercy is expressed in and through transformative acts that alter the person's standing. M38 already analyses the atonement<-divine-compassion link (finding 21139). When M05 is analysed, resolve into a finding: mercy/compassion's structural mechanism for altering human standing is atonement (M38).

new_flags.cluster_link_basis: homed to M44 (yielding has no cluster; researcher 2026-06-02)
new_flags.description: Homed from M38 A7 (DIM-180-001, id 127; reg 180 yielding, no cluster -> M44 Relational Disposition). paradidōmi (G3860) spans 12 distinct inner-being uses: betrayal, Christ's passion, voluntary self-giving, judicial delivering-over, apostolic transmission, discipleship under persecution, stewardship, continual self-entrustment, self-abandonment to desire, divine delegation, discipline for restoration, obedience to tradition. When M44 is analysed, treat as a structured taxonomy of 'giving over / handing on' as an inner-being (relational-disposition) category.

new_flags.cluster_link_basis: homed to M44 (division has no cluster; researcher 2026-06-02)
new_flags.description: Homed from M38 A7 (DIM-52-001, id 146; reg 52 division, no cluster -> M44). Greek division vocabulary (schizō G4977, schisma G4978, merizō G3307, diamerizō G1266, meris G3310, meros G3313) shows a differentiated inner-being landscape: schisma/schizō = communal fracture over identity evaluation; merizō = internal volitional division; diamerizō = interpersonal fracture from ultimate allegiance; meris/meros = relational belonging and volitional orientation toward Christ. When M44 is analysed, examine these terms in relation to each other and to the Hebrew portion vocabulary (chelek, cheleq, chalaq).

Homed from M38 A7 (DIM-52-002, id 147; reg 52 division/portion, unclustered -> M44). Five groups in the division registry carry Divine-Human Correspondence; the portion/lot vocabulary (chelek, chalaq, meros, meris) consistently operates in divine-human correspondence mode — God allots, human receives, inner states arise in response. When M44 is analysed, examine whether this correspondence structure is intrinsic to the portion concept or a feature of the particular corpus.

Homed from M38 A7 (DIM-52-003, id 148; reg 52 -> M44). Mark 1:10 (heavens torn open at Jesus's baptism) appears as a related verse in group 5272-001 (schizō) — a cosmic/physical use of schizō with no inner-being subject in the term itself; may reflect an over-broad Verse Context classification. When M44 is analysed, assess whether the heavens-torn image carries inner-being significance through its context (divine approval, identity revelation) or whether it is a VC classification gap to correct.

fold_findings.resolution_note: M38 side already covered by char-125 finding 21139 (atonement from divine compassion). Generative mercy side routed to M05 via SD_CLUSTER. Folded for M38.
